ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for grape-flavoured Yakult tea blend; closest parent 'tea beverage' (FOODON_03301103) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers tea and tea-based beverages; no specific code for probiotic-grape-tea blend exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 'Tea (substance)' is the closest available parent; no specific concept for Yakult-grape tea blend exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one 400 ml cup as typically served at bubble tea and specialty tea shops in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Brewed green tea (FDC ID 171917), Yakult probiotic drink nutrient profile (USDA Branded Foods), and grape juice (FDC ID 167771); proportions estimated at approximately 60% brewed tea, 25% Yakult, 15% grape juice/syrup with added sugar.
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Grape Yakult Tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or other approved primary sources. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of brewed green/black tea, Yakult probiotic drink, and grape juice in proportions consistent with standard Singapore bubble-tea-style preparation. Energy and sugar are primarily driven by Yakult (approximately 11.4 g sugar per 65 ml bottle) and grape syrup. Values represent a reasonable mid-range estimate per 100 g of the final beverage.

gi_source.title: Fructose-containing beverages and glycaemic index: analogous estimate based on Yakult fermented milk drink (GI ~46) and grape juice (GI ~53) composite; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Grape Yakult Tea. GI estimated at 48 based on a weighted composite of: brewed green/black tea (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate contribution), Yakult fermented milk drink (GI ~46 per University of Sydney GI Database), and grape juice (GI ~53 per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables). Weighted average reflects approximate proportions of the beverage. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 34 g carbohydrate in 400 ml serving) / 100 ≈ 16.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~8 mg per 100 g; ~32 mg per 400 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Grape Yakult Tea is low in sodium (~32 mg per serving) and does not pose a significant hypertension risk, though individuals monitoring fluid and sugar intake should account for its moderate sugar load (~31 g per 400 ml serving).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~16.3 per 400 ml serving) driven by added grape syrup and Yakult sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Grape Yakult Tea with caution; a single 400 ml serving delivers approximately 34 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~16, which can meaningfully raise postprandial blood glucose and should be factored into daily carbohydrate budgets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat and negligible cholesterol; moderate free-sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grape Yakult Tea is very low in saturated fat and cholesterol, posing minimal direct cardiovascular lipid risk, but its moderate free-sugar content (~31 g per serving) warrants moderation in individuals managing cardiovascular risk through dietary sugar restriction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grape Yakult Green/Black Tea is a popular Singaporean specialty tea-shop beverage that combines brewed green tea or black tea with Yakult, a Japanese probiotic fermented skimmed-milk drink, and grape-flavoured juice or syrup. The drink is typically served chilled over ice in a 400 ml cup and is characterised by a sweet-tart grape flavour layered over the mild astringency of tea, with a slightly creamy, tangy undertone from the Yakult. It is a staple offering at Chinese-style bubble tea and specialty tea chains in Singapore, including outlets such as Happy Cup. Nutritionally, the beverage is primarily a source of simple sugars (approximately 31 g per 400 ml serving) derived from the Yakult drink and grape syrup, with modest protein (~1.6 g) from the Yakult dairy component and negligible fat. The tea base contributes polyphenols and trace antioxidants. The glycemic load per serving is moderate (~16), making it a beverage that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose should consume in moderation. It is low in sodium and saturated fat, making it broadly acceptable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management when consumed occasionally. The Yakult component provides Lactobacillus casei Shirota, a probiotic strain, though the clinical significance of probiotic content in this diluted mixed-beverage format is uncertain.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Grape Yakult Tea exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of approved USDA FoodData Central entries for brewed tea, Yakult probiotic drink, and grape juice in proportions consistent with standard Singapore tea-shop preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for Yakult and grape juice. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates; no single approved-source entry covers this exact beverage. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero by formulation — tea, Yakult, and grape juice contain no trans fats). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; Yakult contains negligible cholesterol per USDA Branded Foods data). monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used for estimation. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries. 2. The G/B in the original food name refers to Green/Black tea option. 3. GI set to 48 as an analogous estimate; glycemic_index is not naturally zero as this beverage contains glycemic carbohydrates from Yakult and grape syrup. 4. Allergen note: contains milk derivatives from Yakult (skimmed milk); not suitable for dairy-free or vegan diets. 5. Price SGD 11.40 preserved from field survey source record (Happy Cup - Funan). 6. The Yakult component contributes Lactobacillus casei Shirota probiotic bacteria; clinical significance in this diluted format is uncertain and not reflected in nutrient values.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full composite nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central component entries. Established GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Populated multilingual names and aliases.

Iteration 2: Addressed Q8 failure. Corrected a character discrepancy in a Hindi alias ('अंगूर याकुल्ट चाय' to 'अंगूर याकुल्ड चाय') in the flat 'aliases' list to match 'aliases_by_language.hindi'. Added 'Pútáo Yǎnglèduō Lǜ/Hóng Chá' to the flat 'aliases' list to match 'aliases_by_language.romanized'. Added the canonical 'food_name' ('Grape Yakult Green/Black Tea') to both 'aliases_by_language.english' and the flat 'aliases' list for comprehensiveness. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed trans_fat_g and cholesterol_mg zero justifications present. Confirmed GI > 0 and gi_category = 'low' per ≤55 threshold. Confirmed all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
